title: Hyperon single-particle potentials in nuclear matter based on baryon-baryon interactions derived within chiral effective field theory
author_string: Asanosuke Jinno, Johann Haidenbauer, Ulf-G. Meißner
abstract: An analysis of the Lambda and Sigma single-particle potentials is presented, based on YN interactions derived within chiral effective field theory up to next-to-next-to-leading order (N$^2$LO). The self-consistent Brueckner-Hartree-Fock framework is employed within the continuous choice for the single-particle potential. The result for the Lambda single-particle potential is comparable to the ones obtained with previous chiral YN interactions up to next-to-leading order (NLO). The Sigma single-particle potential is found weakly attractive, in contrast to earlier weakly repulsive results, reflecting new constraints from the recent J-PARC E40 data on $Σ^+p$ scattering. An estimate of the theoretical uncertainty of the single-particle potentials is provided.
comment: 6 pages, 4 figures, 2 tables, conference proceeding of The 15th International Conference on Hypernuclear and Strange Particle Physics (HYP2025)
